Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(October 3rd, 1988) (Rakim Athelaston Mayers, known as A$AP Rocky, is born in Harlem, New York City)
(1996) (At the age of eight, Rocky was inspired by his older Ricky Mayers to start rapping and freestyling)
(1996-1999) (From the ages eight to eleven, Rocky and his family would often move back and forth from Harrisburg, Pennsylvania and Harlem, New York City)
(February 26th, 2002) (Rocky's Mayers brother and inspiration Ricky Black is murdered near their home in Harlem, New York City in a street-related incident)
(Early-Mid 2002) (At the age of thirteen, the passing of Rocky's older brother Ricky Mayers inspired him to take his advice to take rapping more seriously)
(Mid-Late 2002) (When Rocky was thirteen years old he began to hustle selling marijuana as a way to hustle money)
(2003-2004) (By the time Rocky was fourteen and fifteen years old he began to sell crack cocaine in the Bronx)
(Late 2005) (A$AP Mob is founded by A$AP Yams, A$AP Bari and A$AP Illz)
(Late 2005) (Rocky meets members of A$AP at the dry cleaners having a similar interest in skinny jeans, asking what they call themselves)
(2006) (A$AP Mob begins to establish themselves more locally (this is often when the crew is said to be "founded"))
(Early-Mid 2006) (Rocky's childhood friend and Harlem legend Knocka wins a lawsuit, obtaining 1.5 million dollars) 
(Early-Mid 2006) (After winning the lawsuit, Knocka creates a crew named 'MDB' standing for 'Million Dollar Babies')
(Mid-Late 2006) (Close friend Rah Money introduces Rocky to SoHo and gets him into the MDB crew)
Leak Date: Born and raised in Harlem, New York, Rakim Mayers was no stranger to the culture of hip-hop growing up with his name even being named after artist Rakim by his mother, but getting the nickname Rocky at a very young age. While just starting to rap at the young age of eight years old Rocky’s brother Ricky Black had a huge influence on his career and motivated him to keep pursuing music, the same year his dad—who was “into the streets back then”—moved his family to Pennsylvania. His father went to jail for selling drugs when Rocky was 12, and his mother moved the family to North Carolina. Homeless, they ended up living in a shelter. Their luck never got better, and Rocky’s mother moved the family back to New York. Unfortunately, Ricky Mayers then was sadly shot and killed just a block away from where Rocky was born on February 26th, 2002 and this tragic event motivated Rocky to start rapping a bit more seriously at the age of thirteen. Soon enough, while bouncing around homeless shelters in the city with his mother, out of options and tired of grinding it out, Rocky got involved in the drug trade. He started selling weed, and by age 15 had graduated to selling crack in the Bronx.

We met just being different, being some poppin' niggas from the hood. We fucked crazy bitches and were little legends in our own realm. Real recognizes real, and we started doing music together around thirteen or fourteen years old. We go back." It has been confirmed that Rocky was inspired to start taking music more seriously at the age of thirteen when his older brother passed away, so it is possible some of of his earliest recorded tracks from around 2001 and 2002 are with Ferg, but that has not been confirmed as of now.

Leak Date: When Rocky was around 17 Rah Money introduced him to a group started by one of Rocky's closest friends, Knocka. Around the time that Knocka turned 18 years old he started this group when he had gotten a lawsuit, obtaining one million dollars, Knocka named this group "Million Dollar Baby", but was oftentimes stated as "MDB". Rocky has described the group as "really Harlem", and he would often be seen with the likes of Knocka, Rell Money and Rah Money, as this was Rocky's main friend group at the time. In 2007 Rocky joined the A$AP Mob and started going by A$AP Rocky, but was still associated with MDB, but eventually, around 2008 Rodky differed away from MDB and was mainly focused on being a part of A$AP. Seemingly, Rocky did made music during this era but it will likely never be recovered as it is probably only still owned by very few people.

(May 10th, 2010) ("Been Around The World" is released, and featured on the front page of OnSmash.com, giving him a reputation in the Harlem area)
(October 23rd, 2010) (The final 'leak' is posted to the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel, "Bittersweet Symphony Part 2")
Leak Date: Sometime during 2006, while Rocky was still an active member in 'MDB', another Harlem based group was founded, entitled the 'A$AP Mob'. The collective was founded by A$AP Yams, A$AP Bari and A$AP Illz while together in SoHo as Bari explains, "I was like, 'Yo, we're always trying to come up with different names and shit. We should just be called PIF Unit.' The shit was like, Paid In Full, something. So one day, Yams was like, 'Fuck that. We're called A$AP'". The monicker 'A$AP' is an acronym meaning to 'Always $trive And Prosper', a saying Yams had gotten from another forgotten founder of A$AP unfinished WIP. Rocky joined the A$AP Mob in 2007. Rocky met A$AP Yams in 2008. In 2010, Yams would begin to release throwaways on the YouTube channel “HarlemzMostHated" while acting like he didn't know who Rocky was to try and gain fame, which would be featured on a fan-made mixtape titled “Deep Purple” which was released on April 22nd, 2011 and is commonly confused to be Rocky’s first project. While this compilation isn't official, it began to give Rocky more traction with his music.

✨ Been Around The World [V2] [OG File]
(prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
Original Filename: Been Around The World
The original file for the song "Been Around The World" (originally released May 10th, 2010) sent out by A$AP Yams in a email to DJ DP, asking to have the song chopped and screwed on October 15th, 2010. Just five days later on October 20th, 2010 DJ DP would send his remixed version of the file back to A$AP Yams, known as the 'Slow Lane Remix' (a chopped and screwed remix series by DJ DP). That remixed file was then uploaded to A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on the same day the "Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)" was sent back on October 20th, 2010. The original file and the remixes original file was leaked by community member KILLRITE on January 2nd, 2024.

Name:
✨ Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix) [V1] [OG File]
(c&s. DJ DP) (prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
Original Filename: Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)
An official 'Slow Lane Remix' done by DJ DP to the song "Been Around The World" which was originally released on May 10th, 2010. The song was first sent to DJ DP by someone "on Rocky's team", who is rumored to be A$AP Yams, on October 15th, 2010 requesting for the song to be remixed along with stating the song would have a cover art with DJ DP's name on it, then the freshly-made remix was sent back to that person on October 20th, 2010 and was uploaded to A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on the same day. The remix was completely lost until it resurfaced by KILLRITE on January 1st, 2024 with the OG file leaking on January 2nd, 2024 along with the OG file for the non-remix version of the song also by KILLRITE. The remix was reused as track eleven intended for the May 16th, 2011 copy of the May 16th, 2011 copy of Rocky's "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape along with the original song, these were the only 2010 songs on the mixtape. The files that were used on that copy of the album were leaked by KILLRITE and Zanthin on July 18th, 2024 as a part of the successful $250 groupbuy for the "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape.

While the song had long been speculated to exist, its title was officially confirmed by an insider on December 21, 2023. Leak Date: It is not exactly clear when Rocky and fellow artist Seth Narley met, but seemingly through A$AP Bari. The two supposedly moved in together somewhere around 2010. While living together the two up and coming rappers would being to collaborate on a lot of music according to a friend of Seth. What is seemingly the first single for the project titled "Grown Up" was released on December 23rd, 2010 in a video in which it states the song was intended for Seth Narley's "Freshmen N High School" EP, while also showing the tracklist with the song appearing, as well as stating the song would appear on a Rocky mixtape titled "Purple Swagg" which turned out to be fake. A while later another single titled "Grippin' Woodgrain" was released on October 26th, 2011. This single was the last collaboration we would ever see with the duo as Rocky then blew up with "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" and the two went their separate ways. On March 17th, 2024 Seth Narley would confirm in Instagram direct messages that him and Rocky actually had a mixtape planned together with "some releases some not" confirming the previously stated singles would have appeared.

In another interview he mentioned "Get High" and "Toast To The Gods" were on the tracklist. It's unknown what the other songs that would have appeared are but those are lost forever. Name:

(Purple Swagg)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: According to multiple A$AP Bari interviews, Rocky had a whole mixtape before "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" but the engineer Rocky was working with deleted all of the songs and the only songs recovered were "Purple Swag", "Roll One Up" and "Demons". In another interview he mentioned "Get High" and "Toast To The Gods" were on the tracklist. It's unknown what the other songs that would have been but those are lost forever. This mixtape was deleted inbetween March 7th, 2011, the day the "Numb" instrumental was released by Clams Casino, which Rocky would later use to make "Demons" and prior to April 14th, 2011 as that is when "Purple Swag" released as a single and Rocky would not have to recover a released song. It is highly likely this mixtape is titled "Purple Swagg", as in the description of the behind the scenes video for Rocky and Seth Narley's track "Grown Up", which was uploaded on December 23rd, 2010 it states that the song was meant for a Seth Narley mixtape titled "FreshManInHighSchool" and for a apparent Rocky mixtape titled "Purple Swagg". When contacting the person who made the video he stated that "Purple Swagg" was not a real title, but based on the fact that the song "Purple Swag" did not come out until four months later it is possible it was the real title of Rocky's mixtape at the time. It seems like too much a coincidence that the name "Purple Swagg" was used in the description if it was not real, meaning it is likely the name of the deleted mixtape.

Leak Date: Following the deletion of his previous mixtape, Rocky began working on a new project titled "Mouth Fulla Gold" and had a copy of the mixtape ready on May 16th, 2011, which was sent out to select fans by A$AP Yams. During that month, likely around the time “Wassup” was released, Rocky stopped selling drugs and started take his brother's advice and truly started to focus on his rap career. On April 14th, 2011, Rocky released the song "Purple Swag" on the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel. The track did not gain much recognition right away until it was put on the "Deep Purple" compilation as track three and had a music video release on July 5th, 2011, which led to the song becoming his first breakout hit. Rocky would continue to work on "Mouth Fulla Gold" as another copy was put together on June 17th, 2011. The same day the "Purple Swag" music video was released, A$AP Yams took to his now deleted Tumblr "realniggatumblr" and mentioned the name "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" for the first time, which was then due at the end of July 2011.

(October 31st, 2011) (LIVE.LOVE.A$AP is released)
Leak Date: Following the hype and high expectations from the first single's music video, Rocky then released another single on July 21st, 2011 titled “Peso”, another hit under Rocky’s belt. From here, the mixtape was then delayed to August for unknown reasons. Rocky later 'leaked' an early version of "Trilla" on September 17th, 2011. In early October, Rocky signed a revolutionary two-year $3 million dollar record deal. Finally after all the waiting, on Halloween 2011 the project “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” was officially released. This mixtape is widely known for its slowed down, Houstan influenced, psychedelic and dream-like sounds which made it stand out from many other hip-hop projects from this time period and it would become a gigantic pioneer for the genre of cloud rap for many years to come. Much later in 2021 the mixtape was re-released on streaming services, missing certain songs such as "Kissin' Pink" "Out Of This World" and "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" along with featuring different versions of the songs "Leaf" and "Acid Drip", but added a new song titled “Sandman” dedicated to the late A$AP Yams with production by long time collaborator Clams Casino along with a newer producer Kelvin Krash.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:

(Spring 2012) (Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp's friendship ends over beef)
Leak Date: It was revealed in a interview at Ballers Eve on September 14th, 2011 that Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp planned to release a six track collaboration EP titled "B.M.W" under the group name of TWIN meaning "two wild ignorant niggas" as the two would often call each other their twin at the time. On Rocky's debut mixtape SpaceGhostPurrp featured on two songs which were "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" and "Keep It G". Later on, SpaceGhostPurrp would produce Rocky's song "Pretty Flacko", but as April 2012 was underway the two artists started to have some beef as SpaceGhostPurrp claimed Rocky stole his style in music, fashion style among other things such as how the A$AP Mob began to use hieroglyphics just as the SpaceGhostPurrp lead Raider Klan would. He called out Rocky for using lyrics from his song "My Enemie" on his "Goldie" single in April. From there the beef got worse and SpaceGhostPurrp would often diss Rocky while even Rocky would throw disses his way telling him to stick to making beats. On December 17th, 2012 SpaceGhostPurrp released a solo EP with the same title so it's possible some of the songs that appeared on that project were originally for the collab tape. On the deluxe version of Rocky's first studio album the songs "Jodye" and "Angels" have direct disses to SpaceGhostPurrp.

(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(Late October 2011) (The A$AP Mob's LIVELOVEA$AP Tumblr page changed to LONGLIVEA$AP)
(November 29th, 2011) (An A$AP Mob compilation mixtape titled LONG.LIVE.A$AP is mentioned in a HipHopDX interview, which was planned to be released after the deluxe to LIVE.LOVE.A$AP)
(February 2012) (Mixtape is set to release but is delayed to April, Yams claims the compilation is untitled)
(March 2012) (Mixtape is delayed from April to May)
(March 26th, 2012) ("Told Ya" is released, TRILLUMINATI title is revealed, this same week the Mob moves to Los Angeles to work on the project)
(May 2nd, 2012) (A$AP Yams explains there is no motivation to work on the project in Los Angeles so they plan to move back to New York to get inspired)
(May 2012) (Title gets changed to Lord$ Never Worry)
(August 28th, 2012) (Lord$ Never Worry is released)
Leak Date: With Rocky on the rise with massive success from his mixtape “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” it only made sense for the A$AP Mob to all make a project together so more of the other members of A$AP could be recognized for their talents, and they did just that. The project was first announced by Rocky in a November 29th, 2011 interview with HipHopDX with him stating “Yeah, “LONG.LIVE.A$AP.” That’s like the joint with everybody on it. It’s a compilation. I plan on dropping that after “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” drops again, because we gonna do a deluxe version of “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP”. Right after that, we gonna drop the A$AP compilation with everybody on it.” The project has been described as a compilation at the time and the title would later become the name of Rocky’s first studio album. The project was said for a February 2012 release which was eventually pushed back for an April release but by the time March came around a May release was revealed. This was revealed in a Tumblr post from the A$AP Mob speaking on the release of A$AP Ant and Bodega Bamz song “Told Ya” on March 26th, 2012 states “The baby goon of the year A$AP Ant brings you his first visual alongside Bodega Bamz produced by DJ Carnage. Look out for TRILLUMINVTI this May featuring new music from Pretty Flacko, Nasty Baby 90’s Nigga, Twelvy the Good Goon, Fergenstein aka Diamond Dolla$ Page, and the rest of the Mob”. In this post a new name for the project can be seen “Trilluminati” and again, was set to be released in May 2012. A fan asked where the project was in April and Yams responded on his Tumblr on May 2nd, 2012  saying that the A$AP Mob thought moving to LA “would be the move”, but it ended up being the total opposite. He claimed “The energy is dead here there was no motivation here to make music”. He then said they’re going back to New York City to get the inspiration they needed again, Yams stated “the music will be rawer than ever believe that....TRILLUMINATI will be here” again confirming the title. With the project still not coming out in May many still wondered if it would be released any time soon, finally in July Yams announced the first single for the album “Bath Salt” which was released on July 27th. Yams stated there were no more delays and the album would come out on August 28th officially announcing the title “Lord$ Never Worry”. Before this another single “Purple Kisses” released on August 13th and the last single “Work” released on August 20th. Finally, after all the delays “Lord$ Never Worry” released on the planned date of August 28th, 2012.

Missing Original File
Original solo Rocky reference track for the song "Lords Never Worry" recorded during the sessions of the A$AP Mob’s mixtape of the same title. This version of the song does not feature either fellow A$AP Mob members A$AP Twelvyy or A$AP Nast and includes an open verse, and seemingly predates when the other members both recorded their verses during a session with Ricci Riera on May 17th, 2012 (where Rocky was not present). This original version of Rocky’s verse has alternate lines including “Hello white America suck my dick, say lords never worry no hope for the kids, Young Blood was a thug, caught slugs from a Crip” instead of just “No hope for the kids, Young Blood was a thug, caught slugs from a Crip” and also “I’m a trill God, that’s the bottom line” instead of “I’m a young lord, that’s the bottom line”. Following when the song was recorded on by A$AP Twelvyy and A$AP Nast, it was not included on the “Lord$ Never Worry” mixtape that was released just a few months later in August 2012 despite having the same title. Eventually, the song got an official release on February 17th, 2016 on the AWGE SoundCloud account as a part of the "Wavy Wednesdays" series with an additional “Cozy Boys” tag that would have not appeared on the original track from 2012. A snippet of the demo was surfaced on December 14th, 2022 on LEAKED.CX by Blue Powerade, and later on the song was obtained by Attam in late 2023 who planned to leak the song as a bonus in a A$AP Rocky Hub groupbuy before it randomly leaked in full on January 1st, 2024. The original file that leaked is currently lost, if you can help and have the file please contact a Tracker Editor.

Fans were hungry for more solo Rocky music as he had been featuring on a handful of songs up until April 16th, 2012 when the first single for his album "Goldie" released with production from a very well respected producer Hit-Boy. The album was delayed once the July release day hit for unknown reasons, but Rocky still worked on the album with a second single titled "Fuckin' Problems" releasing on October 24th, 2012. This song has the likes of Drake, Kendrick Lamar and 2 Chainz and quickly became Rocky's most popular song at that point, and remains as one of them to this day. It was then announced in December 2012 that the album would release on January 15th, 2013 with the title track "Long Live A$AP" releasing as a single on December 18th, 2012. Just two days later on December 20th, 2012 a listening event for the album took place at a local Harlem music store. The album was completely finished and ready to release on its planned day, this was when just two days after that on December 22nd, 2012 the whole album was leaked online for everyone to hear. Surprisingly, Rocky was not upset by this, infact he was completely okay with it as long as people liked the album as he spent so much time making it. Just four days before the album the last pre-release single titled "Wild for the Night" with Skrillex and Birdy Nam Nam, which was also a huge hit. The album then released on the planned date of January 15th, 2013 along with a deluxe edition adding four more songs to the tracklist. The album is credited for its creative direction as well as big time features after being for the most part unknown not too long before the release. Despite this, Rocky knew the songs such as "Fuckin' Problems" and "Wild for the Night" were made as hits for his record label in a sense and knew he wanted to create a more personal and creative album next.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(January 15th, 2012) (Rocky makes his first beat, "Suddenly")
(January 15th, 2013) (LONG.LIVE.A$AP is released)
(April 12th, 2013) (The mixtape is announced to release summer 2013)
(March 3rd, 2014) ("Riot Rave" and "Unicorn" are released on YouTube)
(April 21st, 2015) (Rocky explains the mixtape is delayed for AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP)
(June 14th, 2019) (In an interview with Kerwin Frost, it is revealed the album was given to Dexter Navy for a passion project, explaining he scrapped the project because he wanted to master production and not have any help from others)
Leak Date: It was revealed in a 2019 interview with Kerwin Frost that Rocky was taught how to make beats in 2011 by close collaborators at the time A$AP Ty Beats and SpaceGhostPurrp, so on his first studio album that was titled  "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" he had a number of production credits. Some songs that he had a large amount of production on is "Suddenly" and "Jodye" which he produced with the likes of Héctor Delgado and Joey Fatts. After the release of that studio album Rocky had a plan to being working on a mixtape full of self-produced instrumentals. This mixtape would not be announced until a interview with WiLD 94.9 on April 12th, 2013 where he said "I'm working on an instrumental album right now. I'm doing all my own beats," he says. "It's going to be interesting to see how people react towards my production." Rocky said he's "not even going to announce it, just drop it" very soon. Although fans would not see a release of the mixtape anytime in 2013 Rocky continued to work on it while touring with the likes of Héctor Delgado, who evidently had a lot of influence on the project. Then later on Rocky would speak on the project in a interview with MTV saying "I think people are gonna be intrigued by it because it's actually good," he continued. "I wouldn't do nothing that I didn't feel confident or comfortable about. I think people gonna like this shit". He revealed the title to be "Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1" and described the mixtape as "really druggy, ambient shit", following up saying "everything is like classical beautiful music and boom-bap Wu-Tang shit mixed together. It's slowed down and it's real vibey, with no lyrics. I feel like it's gonna get people through the summer" stating it would release for free that summer of 2013 with visuals. Nothing happened with the project until February 2014 when Rocky would have riots in New York, getting him banned from filming there for a few years, and he announced a new single "Riot Rave". That song had an accompanied music video promoting Vlone having footage from the riots, and another song released titled "Unicorn" with its own music video in March 4th, 2014 revealing the cover art while simply zooming into different parts of the cover. In the description of the "Unicorn" video it states the album was going to be executive produced by The Dukes, but it is not currently known who they are. There was no word on the album until April 21st, 2015 where Rocky would preview a music video for the album directed by Dexter Navy titled "Chevy", Rocky explained that the instrumental album was delayed because he said he knew fans wanted to hear rapping first, so "AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP" was set to release first. After this, the album was mentioned on "AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP" on the song "Jukebox Joints" where Rocky says he "took the whole year off, just to learn to make beats", but after this there was no mention of the album from Rocky for years. A fan asked Dexter Navy about releasing the "Chevy" music video in 2016, but he said Rocky told him he was not allowed. In a June 2019 interview with Kerwin Frost Rocky revealed the project was scrapped because he wanted to master production and mixing/mastering and essentially not have any help. He said he gave the album to director Dexter Navy for a passion project of his, so it is unknown if Dexter will ever release this.

Name:
L.O.R.D.
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)
(L.O.R.D.S.)
(exec. prod. A$AP Yams)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(August 28th, 2012) (Lord$ Never Worry is released)
(August 26th, 2013) (An untitled A$AP Mob album is announced by A$AP Yams during the VMAs)
(October 15th, 2013) (Album misses its original release date)
(March 4th, 2014) (L.O.R.D. is expected to release but is later delayed till April 2014)
(September 26th, 2014) (A$AP Yams confirms L.O.R.D. is scrapped)
Leak Date: During a A$AP Ferg interview during the VMAs on August 26th, 2013 a upcoming, then untitled, project from the A$AP Mob was revealed by one of the the founders A$AP Yams to be releasing on October 15th, 2013, but on that date Yams revealed that he only revealed the initial release date because Amazon had leaked the target date without it being confirmed. He went on to say the album was pushed back because of features and sample clearances. Then the first single for the album was released on December 4th, 2013 which was “Trillmatic” with A$AP Nast and Method Man with the album's second single coming out not long after this time being titled “See Me” and only featuring A$AP Ant. The album was said to be finished on February 20th, 2014 with a new March 4th, 2014 release date. That release date slowly approached and it eventually became delayed until sometime in April with the album's third single releasing on April 22nd, 2014 titled “Xscape” featuring only A$AP Twelvyy. The album continued to be worked on and the fourth single was released on June 6th, 2014 featuring the likes of A$AP Rocky, A$AP Ferg, A$AP Twelvyy and A$AP Nast titled “Hella Hoes”. After the release of that last single the album’s development went relatively radio silent for the most part until September 26th, 2014 when A$AP Yams confirmed on his Tumblr account that “L.O.R.D.” was officially scrapped. The reasons he gave as for what reason the project was scrapped was there was “too many energies” as it was “hard to get everybody on the same page. He then stated that the best tracks from the album would eventually make their way onto Rocky, Twelvvy and Nast’s solo albums, it’s possible that some made their way onto “AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP” in 2015 after Yams’ unfortunate passing, but that is unclear. The solo project from Twelvvy “12” which was released in 2017 only features one confirmed song from “L.O.R.D.”, and Nast never has released a solo project throughout his entire career. As previously stated, A$AP Yams passed away on January 18th, 2015 and even though “L.O.R.D.” was scrapped the A$AP Mob got together to make the first album in the “Cozy Tapes” series “Cozy Tapes: Vol. 1 Friends -“ on October 31st, 2016.

After this devastating event he changed the entire concept of the album to a new direction; he had some lesser known features such as a homeless man from London named Joe Fox who featured on a plethora of tracks while still having big names such as Kanye West and Lil Wayne. The project again gained attention through its singles such as “L$D” and “Everyday” among many other songs on the album.

Name:
Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends -
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob & AWGE)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(September 26th, 2014) (A$AP Yams confirms L.O.R.D. is scrapped)
(January 18th, 2015) (A$AP Yams passes away)
(May 31st, 2015) (Rocky reveals he is working on a posthumous Yams project)
(January 16th, 2016) (The Wavy Wednesdays series begins)
(March 30th, 2016) (The last Wavy Wednesdays loosie is released)
(October 31st, 2016) (Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends - is released)
Leak Date: Around the time of A$AP Yams' unfortunate death in January of 2015 the A$AP Mob decided to produce an album as a whole group, with this being the first one since 2012’s ‘Lord$ Never Worry’. The title of this project was titled ‘Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends -‘ with the friends in the title signifying the many other collaborators outside of the mob including people such as Tyler, The Creator, Playboi Carti and even Lil Uzi Vert. This project gained lots of success through songs like ‘Telephone Calls’ and ‘Yamborghini High’, two of the singles for the tape, the success of this project motivated the A$AP Mob to release a second volume in the future.

Name:
Telephone Calls [V1]
(with A$AP Rocky & Playboi Carti) (feat. Yung Gleesh) (prod. Plu2o Nash & Héctor Delgado)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The song titled "Telephone Calls" did not always feature Tyler, The Creator as revealed by Rocky himself. He stated him, Lil Yachty, Lil Uzi Vert and Young Gleesh were just hanging out passed out on the floor. That is when Tyler, The Creator walked into the house and said “What the fuck, dude? What’s everyone passed out for? Did you guys have a party or some shit?”. This is also when Tyler was introduced to Lil Yachty as he said “Who’s that guy with the red hair? He sounds like André 3000!” Rocky then said, “That’s Lil Yachty.” He then said, “Lil what?”. Rocky then played "Telephone Calls", and Tyler was interested in the song and looped the “Telephone call from young Carti, said it’s lit” as Rocky revealed he did not record his verse at this point. They then went across the street and recorded Tyler's verse and Rocky likely recorded his as well. There was a snippet of the song played on December 25th, 2015 and this is likely the original version without Tyler, The Creator along with Rocky's verse, but it's unconfirmed.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(May 26th, 2015) (AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP is released)
(September 2016) (Rocky begins work on his next album)
(December 19th, 2016) (Rocky previews " Canary Bricks" and teases the TESTING name for the first time)
Leak Date: Similar to Rocky’s previous solo project he totally changed the direction of his third studio album “TESTING”, for example the well known psychedelic production of Rocky’s music now had a more industrial feel and the style in overall took a big turn compared to his last release. He worked with many new faces such as Dean Blunt and Kelvin Krash for this project making a big difference. The album saw some success through songs such as ‘Praise The Lord (Da Shine)’ which is now Rocky’s most popular song to date, though was not praised as much as his past releases and failed to chart number one on the Billboard 200, which motivated him to change his sound on his next release.

(Late 2016) (Album is conceived)
(November 3rd, 2017) (French Montana says "me and Rocky got a project, we'll be coming out with it real soon", no release ever happens)
(2018) (Album is seemingly set to release again but never does)
Leak Date: Sources have stated that the collaboration project between Rocky and French Montana started out as a single, which was likely ‘Said N Done’, then a mixtape then developed into a full fledged album. It’s been said that “They had a lot of chemistry in the studio”. In August of 2017 French Montana revealed that the project would release very soon and sources stated in November of 2017 that the album would be released by the end of the year. Some of the songs from the project were released later by Montana which were ‘Straight For The Bag’ in 2020 and ‘Chit Chat’ in 2023. Through file names the project is said to be titled "Rocky Montana". It is currently unknown if an album cover exists yet it is likely since the project was heavily considered to be released, the photo used here is from a March 2017 photoshoot which was possibly tied to the album.

It has been stated by Mod that the album was intended to drop with “TESTING” on May 25th, 2018, but the whole album was entirely scrapped just a few weeks before for unknown reasons.

Name:
TESTING [V2]
(exec. prod. A$AP Rocky, AWGE, Chace Infinite & Héctor Delgado)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(August 24th, 2017) (Rocky claims the album is "about to drop" and will "for sure" release by 2018)
(October 29th, 2017) (Rocky performs "Distorted Records" at Camp Flog Gnaw, starting the rollout)
(January 2018) (Rocky flies to Berlin to finish the album)
(May 25th, 2018) (TESTING is released)
Leak Date: Similar to Rocky’s previous solo project he totally changed the direction of his third studio album “TESTING”, for example the well known psychedelic production of Rocky’s music now had a more industrial feel and the style in overall took a big turn compared to his last release. He worked with many new faces such as Dean Blunt and Kelvin Krash for this project making a big difference. The album saw some success through songs such as ‘Praise The Lord (Da Shine)’ which is now Rocky’s most popular song to date, though was not praised as much as his past releases and failed to chart number one on the Billboard 200, which motivated him to change his sound on his next release.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(2017) (Project is conceived during TESTING sessions)
(July 23rd, 2018) ("Potato Salad" releases as a single, album is announced)
(January 22nd, 2019) (Tyler announces the project doesn't exist)
Leak Date: While working on “Flower Boy” in 2017 Tyler, The Creator and A$AP Rocky had the idea to create a collaboration album called ‘WANG$AP’. The two had collaborated on songs in the past and decided to bring their talents together for a album. In late 2018 the two released a single called ‘Potato Salad’. Although this is the only official release we ever got from this project there were more songs made. Tyler stated in early 2019 on twitter “i wish rocky would shut the fuck up and stop always saying what he's working on 40 years before its even a fleshed out idea.” and “that shit dont exist yall =)”. Tyler later revealed in 2025 that him and Rocky as a duo were originally meant to be called "DON'T TAP THE GLASS", a phrase which he would eventually reuse as the name of his ninth studio album.

The song is about Rocky's progression in his life and career and touches on the gradual impact of fame, lifestyle and personal growth. Near the end of the track there is an eighteen second open verse followed by a short ten second verse which is then followed by a fifty second open verse featuring heavy drums not heard on any other part of the song. A snippet was leaked under the name "Don't Be Drugs Pt. 2" on December 11th, 2019, which is a fake title that is referring to another unreleased song titled "Pschodelics" which is also known as "Don't Do Drugs", but this song is completely unrelated. The track was then leaked in full a few weeks later by RyanAllen on December 25th, 2019 where the real title was revealed.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(August 2018) (Album was expected to release but never does)
(December 28-29th, 2019) (Sessions for the album are held)
Leak Date: After the Cozy Tapes Vol. 3 album failed to release during AWGEST 2018, many had lost all hope for this project's release. Later on, A$AP Ant confirmed the album was still in the works on April 20th, 2019 through a Tweet stating “Just got offf phone 12vy, a$ap mob up, cozy tapes 3 coming soon.” Through leaks we can see there were sessions for the album in December 2019 just before Yams Day in January 2020 in which the same month  A$AP Nast Tweeted  "Cozy Tapes 3", but again, with no avail. It was speculated in 2021 after seeing many members of the mob together again after drama within the group's members that the album would still be released. The album wasn’t mentioned again until January 2022 in an interview with A$AP Ant saying that they have to get the chemistry right and that it’s still coming. The project has not seen a release since. Often referred to as “Cozy For Lyfe” but a true title is unconfirmed. The cover used here is promo for Yams Day 2020.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(March 2020) (The COVID-19 pandemic escalates globally, presumably putting delays on any project being worked on)
(Spring-Summer 2020) (During quarantine, Rocky took interest into darker aesthetics, German Expressionism and Tim Burton)

Leak Date: After seemingly scrapping the “ALL $MILE$” concept, Rocky began to work on his newest body of work. The first sign of this new album was when he previewed a song he began to play live at most of his live performances from that point titled “NO I.D. (GRIM FREESTYLE)” on December 22nd, 2020 in collaboration with Marine Serre. The project at that time was called "THE GRIM", as confirmed by producer HItkidd. In an interview with GQ from May 2021 Rocky expressed that he estimated the album was 90% complete, but the album didn’t see a release after that point. In the same interview he described the project as being a “ghetto love tale”.

Name:
🏆🥉 ??? [V1]
(prod. A$AP Rocky & A$AP P On The Boards)
(GRIM, GRIM Freestyle, No I.D (GRIM))
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The original version of the track “GRIM” was recorded as a voice note sometime during the Summer of 2020 (around July-August) when Rocky and fellow A$AP Mob member A$AP P On The Boards were in the studio together. The original creation of this song was spoke about more in detail in an interview with P On The Boards from October 27th, 2024 when he was asked about one of his favorite projects he’s worked on. In the interview he revealed the beat started from a melody Rocky played when he stated, “He was playing a melody on a Mellotron he just got” he continued, “we recorded it on a voice note. The whole process was chaos—he was rushing me to finish the beat while he was freestyling in my ear”. This said freestyle is likely the verse heard during the intro of all future versions of the track, which would seemingly include more lines not used that can be heard in the following version that was accidentally previewed in 2023. From there, P On The Board’s finished off this portion of the interview saying, “In the end, the whole song came together from that voice note. That kind of spontaneous energy is crazy”. It is unknown what the title was at this point in time, the amount of unused lines, or the length.

This version of the track has a normal intro, but the “GRIM” adlib before the drop is delayed, and after the drop a non-rerecorded version of the first verse can be heard until it cuts off into a small open verse. After about seven seconds of open, several lines not heard in any other versions are heard before the track is cut off. It is unknown what the title of the track at this time (dated around July or August 2020) or any differences it may have, but it is likely that it features the full freestyle from the original voice note. It may appear that the track is a higher pitch than normal, but the entire set this night was pitched up that way for likely accidental reasons.

Only two days later the full song was leaked on July 9th, 2022 randomly online alongside the music video that was recorded in Ukraine. A rumor was spread that AWGE director HidjiWorld privately sold the song along with the music video but this has turned out to be false. Despite the song being leaked in full it was performed again a week later at Postivus Festival on July 16th, 2022. In early 2023 what was an early version of the cover art for the song was posted on Rocky's Instagram on merch, but then was later deleted. Later on, Rocky confirmed later in a 2023 interview that the song will not appear on his album due to it leaking, but not too long after stating that he played this version at Rolling Loud Miami 2023, later on after he announced he was delaying DON'T BE DUMB, he dropped the song on August 30, 2024.

Name: GR1M [V3]
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(Mid 2021) (THE GRIM shifts into GRIM)
(May 5th, 2022) ("D.M.B." is released as a single)
(May 29th, 2022) (Rocky shows off the "GRIM" belt for the first time)
(September 24th, 2022) (Rocky performs at Rolling Loud New York 2022, Rocky is late and his show is cut short, GRIM is seemingly scrapped afterwards)
(October-November 2022) (GRIM is reworked into DON'T BE DUMB)
Leak Date: By mid 2021, "THE GRIM" was eventually renamed to simply "GRIM". On May 5th, 2022 a track titled “D.M.B.” was released, an ode to his now wife Rihanna, as he proposed to her in the music video for the track. Not long after they introduced their first born baby RZA Mayers into the world on May 13th, 2022. An interview with DAZED from May 22nd, 2022 Rocky officially confirmed the album’s title would not be “ALL $MILE$” and that the album was being wrapped up with him “shooting so many visuals for it”. In that interview he stated his sound would change from the more industrial sound from his previous album “TESTING” into a more “polished” sound. The project has never seen, as Rocky scrapped the "GRIM" idea all together sometime in October-November 2022 and changed the album to "DON'T BE DUMB" which was announced in December.

Original version of the song "Stop Snitching" titled "All The Way Down" recorded sometime in 2022. This version of the song includes two unused verses, alternate lines and poor mixing. The song has no features, less production and the instrumental has a slower in tempo than the updated “Stop Snitching” versions. The track was primarily produced by Hitkidd, who sampled the song "Give This Love a Try" by Leroy Hutson. The song was offered to the A$AP Rocky Hub community for $1,200 dollars when multiple snippets leaked on January 18th, 2025, revealing a large amount of the song. Name: DON'T BE DUMB [V1]
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(October-November 2022) (GRIM is reworked into DON'T BE DUMB)
(November 22nd, 2022) (DON'T BE DUMB title is revealed after being seen on a hat in a video posted by Knocka)
(December 8th, 2022) (DON'T BE DUMB is announced during Thursday night football)
(January 18th, 2023) ("Same Problems?" releases as a single)
(July 20th, 2023) ("RIOT (Rowdy Pipe'n)" releases as a single, Rocky claims he is in album mode)
(August 2023) (Rocky posts "AWGEST" on X hinting a release, but nothing happened the entire month)
(Late 2023) (Album was set to release, but is delayed for unknown reasons)
Leak Date: Eventually, the GRIM project was scrapped and by 2022 Rocky was seemingly working on a new album. The second single for the album “Shittin’ Me”, a fan-favorite song, released on December 2nd, 2022. Not long after on December 8th, 2022 Rocky stated during a football game “Thursday Night Football, Amazon, my first time back on the stage, my album is done, don’t be dumb, let’s go” officially stating the albums title and the next day he performed for Amazon Music on December 9th, 2022 previewing his next single “Same Problems?” which would officially release on Yams Day, January 18th, 2023. Another single would release on July 20th, 2023 titled “RIOT (Rowdy Pipe’n)” and with an interview with Complex the next day Rocky confirmed plenty of the production that would appear on the album and claimed he’s in “album mode”.

Name: DON'T BE DUMB [V2]
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(Late 2023) (Album was set to release, but is delayed for unknown reasons)
(June 25th, 2024) (Album is reannounced for August 30th, 2024 during AWGE's SS25 runway)
(August 2nd, 2024) ("HIGHJACK" releases as a single)
(August 22nd, 2024) (Album is delayed from August 30th, 2024 to Fall 2024 due to 'LEAKS & SAMPLE CLEARANCES')
(December 10th, 2024) (AWGE sends out an email to fans that bought box sets telling them that their items will be returned and the album will release in 'early 2025')
Leak Date: Since delaying the album, Rocky has now had a second child, Riot Rose, and said in late October 2023 that the album would release by the end of that year, which never happened. Later on throughout 2024, Rocky would release merchandise related to the album in April, then in August confirming the albums original release date at the AWGE SS25 as August 30, 2024 previewing 2 new songs, and releasing a new one "HIGHJACK" before he inexplicitly delayed the album once again due to "SAMPLE CLEAREANCES AND LEAKS", dropping the song Tailor Swif instead on that date, and surprise released Ruby Rosary featuring J. Cole the week after, stating the album will be dropping sometime in Fall 2024. Rocky would later say in an interview that the album features influences from Wu-Tang Clan, Diplomatic Immunity, Tim Burton's films, Memphis hip-hop, Portishead, and 1930's German Expressionist silent films.

Name: DON'T BE DUMB [V3]
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(December 10th, 2024) (AWGE sends out an email to fans that bought box sets telling them that their items will be returned and the album will release in 'early 2025')
(February 18th, 2025) (After an almost month long trial, Rocky is found not guilty for assault against former A$AP member Relli, and immediately posts "DON'T BE DUMB" on X)
(February 28th, 2025) (Rocky claims in an interview that the album is finished, but being mixed and mastered, also mentioning he "just wants to drop it")
(March 16th, 2025) (Rocky performs at Rolling Loud Los Angeles, seemingly starting a new rollout, showing new aesthetics, playing multiple new visuals and previewing three new songs)
(June 27th, 2025) (AWGE SS26 'OBLIGATORY FASHION' fashion show takes place, multiple new songs are previewed)
(July 4th, 2025) ("pray4dagang" is released as a single exclusively on Apple Music, released everywhere the next day)
(July 2025) (Rocky goes on a small European tour, appearing at multiple festivals during the month)
(August 2025) (AWGEST passes with no word on album)
Leak Date: The album was then announced to be delayed to early 2025, and then said to be releasing before Rolling Loud on March 15th. Instead Playboi Carti released his album "MUSIC" on March 14th after much speculation Rocky would do so, after his Rolling Loud Los Angeles performance on March 16th, 2025 many fans believe Rocky is ready to start his rollout after previewing four new songs. And later previewing two more at his second AWGE fashion show.

Name:
Stop Snitching [V3]
(feat. BossMan Dlow) (prod. Hitkidd & Kelvin Krash)
(All The Way Down, Old Jim Jones)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: A finished and reworked version of the song previously previewed in 2023 known as "All The Way Down", with an updated title being "Stop Snitchin" performed live during Rocky's Rolling Loud Los Angeles performance on March 16th, 2025. During the start of the preview, Rocky says the new title of the song, mentioning it is also known as "Old Jim Jones". The song features revamped production and a new intro, using the sample "Give Love a Try" by Leroy Hutson which is also used throughout the track. The track has a new refrain, more lyrics in the hook, additional vocal inflections, and now has a feature from Florida rapper BossMan Dlow who says 2 lines, with any signs of Yoshi24k's contributions to the song not being heard. The track also removes the two original verses with the snippeted verse being replaced with a new one, the snippeted verse in which was heard in the original 2023 snippets, which is how the song became wanted by the community in the first place, and it is currently unknown why it was removed. During the performance, Rocky lifts up his shirt to reveal another shirt with a big stop sign as well as saying "STOP SNITCHIN", which is referenced in the song when he says "bout to buy a stop sign shirt to stop the snitchin", with the obvious intention as a diss towards people snitching in general as well as former A$AP Mob member Relli. The two went to trial with due to a November 2021 altercation where Rocky was accused of shooting Relli during an argument in Hollywood, with Rocky being found not guilty on all charges three years later. The general consensus of the trial is that Relli's motive was to get money from Rocky as he would often lie about the altercation during his testimony, meaning Relli was 'snitching', in this case, for money.

Name:
All Black (Stole Ya Flow) [V4]
(prod. ICYTWAT & Kelvin Krash)
(Stole Ya Flow)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: An otherwise unheard title for the song “Stole Ya Flow” being “All Black (Stole Ya Flow)” was seen in a AWGE SS26 ‘OBLIGATORY FASHION’ promotional showcase video shared on July 16th, 2025 in a fictional news article speaking about several key moments in Rocky’s “DON’T BE DUMB” album rollout up to that point. The article specifically mentions Rocky’s performance of multiple tracks, including “All Black (Stole Ya Flow)”, at Rolling Loud Los Angeles performance on March 16th, 2025 saying “At Rolling Loud California in March, Rocky previewed three new tracks — “Your Honor”, “Stop Snitching”, and “All Black (Stole Ya Flow)” — while parachuting onto the stage from a helicopter”. This statement uses this new title rather than using the “Stole Ya Flow” name all other know versions of the song uses for unknown reasons, but the title of the song was “Stole Ya Flow” at Rocky’s Lollapalooza performance (August 3rd, 2025) and his Camp Flog Gnaw performance (November 23rd, 2025) as confirmed by leaked setlist photos. The song has famously been fan-titled “All Black” since its initial debut at Rocky’s Rolling Loud Miami performance on July 24th 2023, and often still gets referred to with that title in the community. The “All Black (Stole Ya Flow)” title also appeared on the fan-edited Genius tracklist for “DON’T BE DUMB” at this time. Due to this, it is been rumored that the article has a flaw (likely from lack of research from whoever wrote it) that went unnoticed.

It's unconfirmed whether it's Gemstones, The GRIM, or Gemstones Its THE GRIM as it was previously, but "Its" will be omitted for the time being.
